steps.title: Identify the source population
steps.content: A random sample can be generalized only to the population from which it was selected. Here, the $90$ students were randomly selected from one specific high school, so the random selection supports conclusions about that school population. The experiment's tasting conditions do not widen that source population.

steps.title: Set the valid scope
steps.content: The sample was not randomly selected from all high schools in Michigan, and the sample itself is not the largest population. The $36$ students are only a subgroup of the sample, so that subgroup is narrower than the original sample. The largest valid population is all students from that high school. The answer is C.
